4992.built_by: A. Marti (Leningrad, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 194 and Dalzavod (Vladivostok, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 202

4993.history: Baltic fleet. Work on this boat was suspended on 25 June 1941. Work was resumed in 1945. Stricken on 12 February 1960.

5007.history: Pacific fleet. Lost in the Sea of Japan 15 / 16 December 1952.

5019.history: Pacific fleet. Decommissioned on 9 November 1956. Stricken on 3 March 1993.

5020.built_by: Baltiyskiy Zavod (Leningrad, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 189 and Dalzavod (Vladivostok, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 202

5024.built_by: A. Marti (Leningrad, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 194 and Dalzavod (Vladivostok, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 202

5025.built_by: A. Marti (Leningrad, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 194 and Dalzavod (Vladivostok, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 202

5026.built_by: A. Marti (Leningrad, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 194 and Dalzavod (Vladivostok, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 202

5027.built_by: A. Marti (Leningrad, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 194 and Dalzavod (Vladivostok, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 202

5028.built_by: A. Marti (Leningrad, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 194 and Dalzavod (Vladivostok, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 202
5028.history: Destroyed by explosion of own torpedo compartment at Nikolayevsk-na-Amure. 17 men lost. Not repaired. Submarine SC-118 was also damaged by this explosion.

5029.history: Northern fleet > Pacific fleet. ShCh-423 left the Northern fleet in early August 1940 to join the Pacific fleet through the Northern route. Assisted by icebreakers she arrived in the far east in mid-October. Renamed ShCh-139 on 17 April 1942. On 26 April 1945 ShCh-139 sank following an explosion on board. On 7 May the submarine was raised and later repaired.

5030.history: Surrendered to Germans at Florø. In German service named UC-2.

5031.history: Pennant number H 48 was used after her escape to Britain in 1940. Decommissioned on 10 March 1944. Returned to Norway in 1945 and converted into an ASW frigate in 1948. Scrapped in 1959. Scrapped

5032.built_by: A. Marti (Leningrad, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 194 and 61 Kommunara (Nikolajev, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 200
5032.history: Black Sea fleet. Stricken on 29 December 1955.

5033.built_by: A. Marti (Leningrad, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 194 and 61 Kommunara (Nikolajev, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 200
5033.history: Black Sea fleet. Decommissioned on 17 February 1956. Stricken on 16 July 1957.

5034.built_by: Baltiyskiy Zavod (Leningrad, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 189 and 61 Kommunara (Nikolajev, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 200
5034.history: Black Sea fleet. Sunk about 25 nautical miles west of Yevpatoriya, Crimea in position 45º12'N, 32º47'E by torpedo of ultra-small Italian submarine CB 4. All hands lost.

5035.history: Black Sea fleet. Most likely sunk on 6 December 1941 off the Bulgarian coast south-south-east of Varna by a Bulgarian aircraft. In 1984 the wreck was found in position 42º53'N, 28º03'E.

5036.history: Black Sea fleet. Stricken on 11 September 1954.

5037.history: Black Sea fleet. Sunk on 9 July 1941 by Rumanian warships near Mangalia in position 43º51'5N, 28º45'E. Might also be mined near Konstanza in July 1941.

5038.history: Black Sea fleet. Stricken on 16 July 1957.

5039.history: Black Sea fleet. Lost after 23 August 1942 most likely due to mines off the Romanian / Bulgarian coast. All hands lost.

5040.history: Black Sea fleet. Decommissioned on 7 February 1956. Stricken on 17 December 1992.

5041.history: Black Sea fleet. Lost near Cape Shabla, Bulgaria after 12 March 1942. Most likely in Romanian minefield S-15.

5042.history: Black Sea fleet. Lost after 14 November 1941 near Cape Akburnu south of Varna, Bulgaria. Most likely mined in Romanian minefield S-18. All hands lost.

5043.history: Black Sea fleet. Most likely lost after 11 December 1942 off the Romanian coast in a Romanian minefield. 44 men lost (all hands).

5044.history: Black Sea fleet. For years it was believed that this submarine was most likely sunk on 14 October 1942 by German auxiliary submarine chaser UJ-116/Xanten off Tulcea, Romania in position 44º39'N, 29º05'E. All hands lost. She might also be lost in a Romanian defensive minefield during October 1942. This last seems to be the case as the wreck was found off Constanta during the summer of 2010 by the Black Sea Wreck Divers team together with the Aquarius Diving Center from Constanta, more info to follow.

5045.history: Black Sea fleet. Torpedoed and sunk by the Italian torpedo boat MAS-571 near Cape Ai-Todor while performing transport duties to Sevastopol. 38 men lost, 2 men survived.

5046.history: Black Sea fleet. Stricken on 29 December 1955.

5047.history: Black Sea fleet. Sunk by German auxiiary submarine chaser UJ 103 and UJ 106 west of Sevastopol, Crimea in position 44º37'N, 32º04'E. All hands lost.

5048.history: Baltic fleet. Lost on 28 August 1941 on the route from Tallinn to Kronshtadt due to mines in position 59º52'N, 25º16'E. 39 men were lost, 2 survived.

5049.history: Baltic fleet. Most likely lost in the Gulf of Finland between 11 and 14 October 1942. Possibly by floating mines or by an attack of Finnish aircraft. All hands lost.

5050.history: Baltic fleet. Decommissioned on 12 September 1945. Stricken on 11 September 1954. Scrapped in 1961.

5051.history: Baltic fleet. Most likely mined in the Gulf of Finland late October, early-mid November 1942. The last contact with the boat was on 29 October 1942.

5052.history: Baltic fleet. Rammed and sunk by the Finnish submarine Vetehinen off Aland, Finland in position 60º03'N, 19º12'E.

5053.history: Baltic fleet. Lost after 12 November 1942 when she sent her last signal, possibly by mines. All hands lost.

5054.history: Baltic fleet. Decommissioned on 23 April 1948. Stricken on 8 April 1957.

5055.history: Baltic fleet. Went missing in the Baltic in October 1942, possibly torpedoed on 26 October 1942 in the Södra-Kvarken channel by the Finish submarine Iku-Turso.

5058.history: Baltic fleet. Sunk by Finnish guard boats VMV-13 and VMV-15 off Teikarsaari Island. All hands lost.

5059.history: Northern fleet. Rammed and sunk by the Soviet fishing trawler RT-43 at the entrance of Kola Bay. 10 crewman (another sourse says 7) survived, including the commander. She sank in 3 minutes to a depth of 250 meters.

5060.history: Northern fleet. ShCh-421 was mined on minefield Ursula on 8 April 1942 and heavily damaged. All power was lost and the submarine was near enemy shores. The crew made a sail of canvas cover and managed to sail out of the hazardous area. All crew was taken to K-22 on 9 April 1942 and after this K-22 sank ShCh-421 by torpedo. Nobody was lost.

5061.history: Northern fleet. Most likely mined in a German defensive minefield off northern Norway during July 1943.

5062.history: Completed in February 1938. Displacement: 1896 GRT Armamant: 8 4" AA guns (4x2), 8 2pdr AA (2x4) In August 1940 Palomares was requisitioned by the Royal Navy and converted to Anti-Aircraft ship. In December 1942 she was converted to a fighter-direction ship, fitted with radar to direct fighter aircraft. Returned to her owners in 1946.
commanders: A/Capt. (retired) John Henry Jauncey, RN (30 Apr 1941 - mid 1944)
A/Lt.Cdr. Robert Jerome Miller, RD, RNR (mid 1944 - 16 Feb 1945)
Cdr. Charles Leigh de Hauteville Bell, DSC, RD, RNR (16 Feb 1945 - ca.late 45)

5064.history: Baltic fleet. The cause of loss of ShCh-317 is often given as the following. While returning from patrol on 14 July 1942 ShCh-317 was located by German patrol boats while crossing "Nashorn" minefield. The Germans attacked ShCh-317, causing some damage. The submarine escaped, but leaving behind a fuel trail, that was followed by the Finnish minelayer Ruotsinsalmi and patrol boat VMV-6. It's very likely that on 14 July depth charges from these two boats sank ShCh-317 with all hands. ShCh-317 last communication was on 10 July 1942 at 0230 hours (Moscow time) while off the south tip of Öland, Sweden. All ten torpedoes aboard had been fired and the submarine was returning to base.

5066.history: Baltic fleet. Lost after 29 September 1941. Reason unknown but most likely mined. All hands lost.

5067.history: Baltic fleet. Departed base on 1 October 1942, most likely mined in the Gulf of Finland.

5069.history: Baltic fleet. Lost, most likely mined, on or after 11 October 1941. All hands lost.

5070.history: Baltic fleet. Mined and sunk off Petershof, in the Kronstadt area. Raised in 1944 and scrapped.

5071.history: Baltic fleet. Lost after its last communication on 5 November 1941. Reason unknown but most likely mined near Tallinn, Estonia. All hands lost.

5072.history: Northern fleet. Last reported off the Tanafjord on 23 April 1943, lost on after this date. All hands lost.

5073.history: Most likely sunk by mistake by Soviet Boston aircraft off the Tanafjord near Gamvik, Norway on 21 September 1944. All hands lost.

5074.history: Northern fleet. Lost after 2 Oct 1943. Was to patrol of the Tanafjord from 4 October to 17 October. Most likely mined.

5076.history: Baltic fleet. Departed base on 11 June 1942 for her first war patrol. ShCh-405 was lost after passing Shepelevski lighthouse, in the Tiger minefield west of Seskar Island in pposition 60º01'N, 28º13'E. All hands lost.

5077.history: Baltic fleet. Lost in the Gulf of Finland in early June 1943. Possibly sunk by German small craft after being damaged by a German aircraft on 1 June.

5079.history: Baltic fleet. Most likely sunk on 26 May 1943 by German small craft in the Gulf of Finland.

5080.history: ShCh-409 was renamed to ShCh-413 on 16 May 1941. Work on this boat was suspended on 25 June 1941 and not resumed.

5081.history: ShCh-410 was renamed to ShCh-414 on 16 May 1941. Work on this boat was suspended on 25 June 1941 and not resumed.

5082.history: Baltic fleet. Work on this boat was suspended on 25 June 1941. Work was resumed in 1944. Stricken on 3 July 1960.

5083.history: Baltic fleet. Most likely mined and sunk off Hanko in mid-September 1941.

5084.history: Baltic Fleet. Decommissioned on 10 August 1944.. Stricken on 20 June 1956.

5085.history: Baltic fleet. Decommissioned on 14 August 1944. Stricken on 2 June 1952.

5086.history: Baltic fleet. Scuttled at Libau (Libava in Russian) to prevent her capture by the Germans.

5087.history: Baltic fleet. Most likely sunk on 3 January 1940 when she hit a mine (laid by the Finnish minelayer Louhi) off Märket.

5088.history: Baltic fleet. Sunk by artillery, hand grenades and depth charges of the German motor torpedo boats S-60 and S-35 near Ujava beacon. S-3 had been refitting at Libau (Libava in Russian) at the outbreak of the war and was unable to submerge. Nevertheless, her Commanding Officer refused to destroy the boat, took the crew of S-1, workers of naval yard (nearly 100 men in total) and tried to burst through to Ust-Dvinsk at night on 24 June. Unfortunately, the slowly moving (5 knots at best) submarine was spotted by two German motor torpedo boats. Their first torpedo salvo missed and the opponents engaged each other in an artillery duel. The motor torpedo boats had quick-firing 20 mm automatic cannons and, utilizing their preference, raked S-3 crew with fire (the reciprocal fire caused light damage). Then the German crews attacked the submarine with hand grenades and finally S-60 dropped one depth charge just ahead the sub's course (and, maybe, fired two torpedoes, that tore S-3 into three pieces). After the submarine had sunk, The Germans took some survivors on board. Some sources says that 20 people were taken by Germans, another reports only 3 survivors, and some sources show that most of the survivors were machine-gunned in water.

5089.history: Baltic fleet. S-4 is most likely rammed and sunk during the night of on 6/7 Janaury 1945 by the German torpedo boat T 3 in Danzig Bay in position 54.93N, 19.39E.

5090.history: Baltic fleet Hit a mine and sunk some 33 nautical miles north-east of Tallinn, Estonia in position 59°48N, 25°32E at 2011 hours on 28 August 1941. S-5 left Reval bound for Kronstadt. Only 9 crewmen survived.

5091.history: Baltic fleet. Went missing on patrol after 6 August 1941. Mined off Öland, Sweden. The wreck was found in 2012.

5092.history: Baltic fleet. S-7 was torpedoed and sunk at 2041hours on 21 October 1942 south-west of Aland in position 59º51'N, 19º32'E. She was located by the Finnish submarine Vesihiisi 8000 meters away. A single torpedo was fired from 2000 meters, hitting the Soviet boat in the stern and sinking it. Only the four men standing on the bridge, including the Commanding Officer survived, and were made POWs. 44 men were lost with the ship. The wreck was discovered in July 1998 by a Swedish diving team. For photographs of the wreck see this website (offsite link, the website itself is in Swedish).

5093.history: Baltic fleet. Left base on 11 October 1941. Believed mined and sunk off Suursaari between 12-14 October 1941. S-8's wreck was however found in 1999 in position 56º10'7"N, 16º39'8"E. All 48 members of her crew went down with the ship.

5094.history: Baltic fleet. Listed as missing in August 1943. Possibly struck a mine near Lavensaari or in Narvski bay. 45 (all hands) lost. The body of a crewmember was found on 4 September on the shore of Seskar island.

5095.history: Baltic fleet. Most likely mined and sunk in the Irben strait on or around 28 June 1941. All hands (40) lost.

5096.history: Baltic fleet. Hit a mine and sunk off Hiiumaa Island, Estonia some 5 nautical miles west of Emmaste in position 58º41'3"N, 22º25'4"E on 2 August 1941. 3 crewmembers managed to raise from 11 m depth through a stern torpedo tube. The others (44 men) went down with the ship. The wreck was found in 1949, raised in 1955, and broken up at Riga during 1957-1958.

5097.history: Baltic fleet. Most likely lost on or after 1 August 1943 north off Naissaar Island. Possibly due to mines of the Nashorn minefield. All hands (46 men) lost.

5098.history: Decommissioned on 07 September 1954. Stricken on stricken 17 December 1956.

5101.history: Northern fleet. Decommissioned on 29 December 1955. Stricken on 12 March 1958.

5102.history: Northern fleet. Decommissioned on 10 December 1955. Sunk as target on 10 October 1957.

5103.history: Black Sea fleet. Decommissioned on 14 March 1955. Stricken on 14 March 1975.

5104.history: Black Sea fleet. Lost with all hands during support duties to Sevastopol. Most likely bombed and sunk by a German aircraft on 26 June 1942 south of the Crimea in position 44º12'N, 33º48'E. Sometimes the Italian small submarines CB-3 or CB-4 are credited for sinking S-32, but dates of their activities do not match with S-32 trip.

5105.history: Black Sea fleet. Decommissioned on 14 March 1955. Stricken on 3 January 1978.

5106.history: Black Sea fleet. Lost after 8 November 1941 off the Bulgarian coast near Sizopol. Possibly lost on mines on 12/13 November off Cape Emine. Two crewmembers bodies were found on 14 November 1941 on the Bulgarian shore. All hands (51 men) lost.

5107.history: Incompolete hull evacuated from Nikolayev in mid-August 1941 and work suspended. Incomplete hull returned to Nikolayev in 1946 and the work resumed. Decommissioned on 17 February 1956. Stricken on 15 March 1962.

5108.history: Scuttled incomplete at Nikolayev on 15 August 1941 to prevent her capture by the Germans.

5109.history: Scuttled incomplete at Nikolayev on 15 August 1941 to prevent her capture by the Germans.

5110.history: Scuttled incomplete at Nikolayev on 15 August 1941 to prevent her capture by the Germans.

5111.built_by: Baltiyskiy Zavod (Leningrad, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 189 ; Dalzavod (Vladivostok, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 202
5111.history: Pacific fleet, later Northern fleet. Decommissioned on 7 September 1954. Stricken on 11 February 1972. Became a memorial in 1973.

5112.built_by: Baltiyskiy Zavod (Leningrad, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 189 ; Dalzavod (Vladivostok, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 202
5112.history: Pacific fleet. Stricken on 24 August 1954 and transferred to China.

5113.built_by: Baltiyskiy Zavod (Leningrad, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 189 ; Dalzavod (Vladivostok, U.S.S.R.) / Yard 202
5113.history: Pacific fleet. Stricken on 24 August 1954 and transferred to China.

5114.history: Pacific fleet, later Northern fleet. Lost by unknown reasons with all hands (50 men) in the Kongsfjord area in March 1944. Most likely mined. S-54 had departed base on 5 March 1944.

5115.history: Pacific fleet, later Northern fleet. Lost by unknown reasons with all hands (52 men). Possiby sunk during an attack by German submarine chasers near Gambiki on 4 December or mined.

5116.history: Pacific fleet, later Northern fleet. Decommissioned on 14 March 1955. Stricken on 9 May 1975 and became a museum ship at Vladivostok (Korabelnaya embankment).

5117.built_by: At.&Ch de la Loire (St. Nazaire, France)
5117.history: Baltic fleet. Ronis was commissioned in the Latvian Navy in 1927 but taken over by the Soviets on 13 August 1940. Ronis was scuttled at Libau to prevent her capture by the Germans.

5118.history: Baltic fleet. Spidola was commissioned in the Latvian Navy in 1927 but taken over by the Soviets on 13 August 1940. Spidola was scuttled at Libau to prevent her capture by the Germans.

5119.history: Northern fleet. Stricken on 17 February 1956. Scrapped in 1957
